name: Ceara (Brazil)

colors.symbolism: peace; field of the central roundel bearing the arms

description: A green field charged with a large yellow lozenge enclosing a white disc that bears the coat of arms of the State of Ceara, in the manner of the Brazilian national flag.
history: Adopted on 25 August 1922; the design follows the pattern of the Brazilian national flag, replacing the celestial sphere with the state coat of arms on a white field.

features.symbolism: echoes the yellow lozenge of the Brazilian national flag, signalling Ceara as a Brazilian state

features.role: white roundel hosting the coat of arms

features.symbolism: Centred on the white roundel, the state arms include a star, a heron, a jangada raft, sugar-cane and cotton, with the date 25 March 1822 commemorating the proclamation of independence in the province
